blockingIssues.description: The after-commit dispatch change fixes the prior email-before-commit leak, but restorePublicResetStateAfterDispatchFailure() can still strand the user without any usable reset token if post-commit cleanup of the new token fails, and the updated AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT assertion now codifies that regressed state instead of preserving prior-token usability.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a merge-gate evidence note that when gate-fast eligibility is satisfied via explicit Surefire includes outside the truthsuite tree, workers must cite the pom include diff and gate output directly because catalog-validation.json and flake-guard.json only inspect the truthsuite root.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: scripts/gate_fast.sh passes TRUTH_TEST_ROOT=erp-domain/src/test/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/truthsuite to validate_test_catalog.py and check_flaky_tags.py, while this packet makes AUTH-09 and ADMIN-14 gate-fast eligible by adding explicit non-truthsuite Surefire includes in erp-domain/pom.xml.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that workers on gate-only packets must either perform every prescribed compile/shared-guidance review step or record explicit deviations in handoff.skillFeedback rather than leaving followedProcedure=true by default.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The auth-merge-gate-regression-coverage review found no product defect, but its transcript omitted a compile command and frontend-v2 review while the handoff still claimed followedProcedure=true.

rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because the underlying AUTH-09 fix track still has a blocking persistence-ordering defect; shared knowledge should not be refreshed until the product defect is fully closed.
